research_answer: The contrast is dramatic. Between 1975 and 2000, Peru made minimal movement through the product space despite experiencing a massive export collapse and recovery that might have been expected to force structural transformation. Malaysia, starting from a broadly comparable developing-country position in 1975, moved significantly and purposively through the product space over the same period — upgrading into more sophisticated and diverse products. This comparison highlights that Peru's export recovery was a restoration of the status quo rather than a structural upgrade, while Malaysia used the same timeframe to fundamentally transform its export composition.
supporting_quotes: Compare this to Malaysia, which has moved significantly and purposively through the product space in the same period.
